seoTitle: How to Configure Screens and Screen Schemes in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to set up Jira screens and screen schemes to control which fields appear when creating, editing, or viewing issues.
intro: Screens control exactly which fields show up when someone creates, edits, or views an issue, and screen schemes tie those screens to specific issue types. This guide covers configuring both from scratch.

steps.heading: Open the screens settings
steps.body: Go to Jira administration and find the Screens section, where existing screens are listed and managed.

steps.heading: Create or edit a screen
steps.body: Build a new screen or modify an existing one, adding and removing fields to match what that step of the issue lifecycle needs.

steps.heading: Create a screen scheme
steps.body: Go to Screen Schemes and define which screen applies to creating, editing, and viewing an issue.

steps.heading: Assign the screen scheme to an issue type
steps.body: Link the screen scheme to a specific issue type through an issue type screen scheme.

steps.heading: Apply it to your project
steps.body: Associate the issue type screen scheme with the relevant project so the configured screens take effect.

faq.question: What's the difference between a screen and a screen scheme?
faq.answer: A screen defines which fields appear, while a screen scheme decides which screen is used for creating, editing, or viewing an issue.

faq.question: Can different issue types use different screens?
faq.answer: Yes, that's exactly what issue type screen schemes are for, mapping different screens to different issue types.

faq.question: Do I need admin access to change screens?
faq.answer: Yes, configuring screens and screen schemes requires Jira administrator permissions.

faq.question: Will changing a screen affect existing issues?
faq.answer: Existing issue data isn't lost, but fields removed from a screen simply won't be visible or editable going forward.
